explanation: The lines inside a function may be out of order.
output:
def __init__(
    self,
    timeout: Optional[float],
    *,
    loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None,
) -> None:
    self._timeout = timeout
    # type: Optional[asyncio.Task[Any]]
    if loop is None:
        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    else:
        warnings.warn(
            """The loop argument to timeout() is deprecated.""", DeprecationWarning
        )
    self._cancel_handler = None  # type: Optional[asyncio.Handle]
    self._loop = loop
    self._cancelled = False  self._task = None
    self._cancel_at = None  # type: Optional[float]
rewrite:
def __init__(
    self,
    timeout: Optional[float],
    *,
    loop: Optional[asyncio.AbstractEventLoop] = None,
) -> None:
    self._timeout = timeout
    # type: Optional[asyncio.Task[Any]]
    if loop is None:
        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    else:
        warnings.warn(
            """The loop argument to timeout() is deprecated.""", DeprecationWarning
        )
    self._cancel_handler = None  # type: Optional[asyncio.Handle]
    self._loop = loop
    self._cancelled = False  self._task = None
    self._cancel_at = None  # type: Optional[float]
